data.description:
## Context
Durations profiling of the full suite (1482 tests, 16m34s). The scripts tier is ~67% of wall time; 37 of the slowest 40 are bash suites.

## Findings
Slowest: test-ticket-transition.sh 58.6s (90 write ops), test-ticket-write-commit-event.sh 44.3s, test-ticket-link.sh 32.9s (97 ops), test-ticket-lib/create/edit ~27s.

ROOT CAUSE — subprocess fan-out per operation. One rebar WRITE = cli.py python+import (~0.05s) -> bash dispatcher -> ~7 python3 heredocs (ticket-create.sh alone has 7), each a fresh interpreter, several importing the reducer -> flock + git commit. ~0.6s/write, ~0.18s/read. Suites do 25-97 ops => 15-58s. This is architectural (the bash engine), not a single bug.

REMEDY — primarily the bash->Python strangler-fig migration (epic adult-oxide-slave, Tiers A-E): collapsing per-command python3 heredocs into one in-process command removes most interpreter starts. Quick wins short of the full migration: consolidate multiple python3 heredocs within a single hot command (create/transition/link) into one python3 call; consider dropping the redundant cli.py python layer for pure bash passthroughs.

SEPARATE FINDING — reconcile retry-backoff in tests. test_mcp.py::test_reconcile_cap0_modes_allowed_in_both_gates uses _loud_acli (acli exits 99); acli-integration._call_with_backoff retries 5x with exponential backoff (2s base) => ~13.6s per test for a gate check that doesn't need a successful fetch. Quick win: use _empty_acli (fast success) for cap-0 gate tests, or cap retries via env in tests.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Decision recorded: which quick wins to take now vs defer to adult-oxide-slave
- [ ] cap-0 reconcile gate tests no longer pay full retry-backoff (use empty-acli or capped retries)
- [ ] Hot write commands' python3-heredoc count reduced where cheap, OR explicitly deferred to the strangler-fig tiers with a note
data.title: Investigation: slow bash-suite tests (subprocess fan-out) + reconcile retry-backoff
